# Cowboy Quick Shot

> A Western-themed reflex game for iOS that measures your reaction time in milliseconds. Hold your iPhone pointed downward like a holster, wait for the draw signal, then flip your phone upward as fast as possible. Built by HarmonyAstroApps.

Cowboy Quick Shot is a motion-controlled iOS game. It is not a photo app, not a camera app, and not a Western storytelling game — it is a focused reflex measurement game with a Wild West theme. The core loop: holster your phone, wait for a randomized "draw" signal, rotate the phone upward, get your reaction time.

## What the app does

- Measures full-body visual reaction time using Core Motion (accelerometer + gyroscope)
- Displays your time in milliseconds with false-start detection
- Tracks personal bests and round history
- Supports two-player duels on separate phones
- Plays Western-themed sounds and animations
- Works offline for solo play and local duels

## Who it is for

- Casual gamers who enjoy short, high-replay games
- People curious about their own reaction time
- Friends who want a quick competitive 30-second challenge
- Sport/esports players looking for a more realistic reflex benchmark than tap tests

## Why motion matters

Cowboy Quick Shot uses motion sensors instead of taps, so the measurement captures the full perception-decision-movement chain (~250 ms average) rather than just the perception-decision phase (~200 ms on tap tests). That makes the numbers slightly slower than tap-based tests but more honest about real-world reflex.

## Platform and pricing

- Platform: iOS (iPhone), iPhone 6s or newer, iOS 15+
- Price: Free
- Developer: HarmonyAstroApps
- Contact: harmonyastroapp@gmail.com

## Benchmarks

- Average human visual reaction time: ~250 ms
- Good player: 220–240 ms
- Fast player: 180–200 ms
- Elite (athlete-level): 150–180 ms
- Below 150 ms: usually false start (anticipation)
